messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is open
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

We are pleased to announce an upcoming livestream seminar designed to enhance your data analysis capabilities. Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 16, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This seminar provides researchers with the practical skills to implement advanced clustering algorithms and dimensionality reduction techniques using R and Python. By mastering methods like PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP, you can effectively resolve the curse of dimensionality and extract meaningful patterns from complex genetic datasets. These powerful unsupervised learning workflows will enable you to translate high-dimensional biological data into robust, publication-ready scientific insights.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/clustering-high-dimensional-data-30) to join the livestream, and please pass this along to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling in R
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Enhance your quantitative toolkit for analyzing complex biological and genetic relationships. Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 11 with Holmes Finch (Department of Educational Psychology - Ball State University). This seminar provides the theoretical and practical foundations to specify and estimate complex, non-linear relationships among latent and observed variables using R and Mplus. Geneticists can apply these advanced modeling techniques to map intricate pathways, gene-environment interactions, and non-linear growth trajectories in phenotypic data. By integrating these robust structural equation models into your R workflows, you can better capture the biological realities of complex traits and accelerate your genomic research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/nonlinear-structural-equation-modeling) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 starts soon
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 starts soon
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Enhance your analytical toolkit with advanced methods for managing complex, multi-dimensional datasets. Clustering High-Dimensional Data 3.0 is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 16 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This upcoming livestream seminar provides comprehensive training in advanced clustering techniques and dimensionality reduction using R and Python. Genetics researchers can apply these methods to extract meaningful patterns from complex, high-dimensional genomic datasets. By mastering algorithms like DBSCAN and UMAP, you can significantly enhance your statistical workflows and accelerate discoveries in computational biology.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/clustering-high-dimensional-data-30) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Developing R/Bioconductor packages
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce the online course ?Developing R/Bioconductor packages for genomics?, taking place 6?10 July (2?6 pm Berlin time).
 
Course website:[ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-packages/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-packages/ ) 
 
 
This 5-day hands-on course is designed to make R package development and the Bioconductor ecosystem accessible to biologists and early-stage bioinformaticians who want to move from package users to package developers. The focus is on building robust, reproducible, and distributable genomic software in R.
 
Participants will learn how to:
Develop well-structured R functions and packages
Document, test, and maintain code effectively
Manage dependencies, versions, and continuous integration (GitHub Actions)
Build Bioconductor-compliant packages and objects
Create vignettes and package websites
Submit, release, and disseminate packages (including JOSS and rOpenSci pathways)
The course combines short lectures (~2h/day), live demonstrations, and extensive guided exercises with solutions. Daily office hours and a dedicated Slack channel will support interaction and Q&A.
 
 
Best regards,
 
The Physalia-courses website
 
[ www.physalia-courses.org ]( http://www.physalia-courses.org )
 
 
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Analyzing Latent Variable Mixture Models
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Enhance your statistical toolkit with advanced methods for modeling unobserved population heterogeneity. Latent Variable Mixture Models is a 2-day seminar livestreaming July 6?7, led by Holmes Finch (Department of Educational Psychology - Ball State University). Researchers analyzing complex genetic datasets can apply these advanced modeling techniques to identify unobserved subpopulations and model nonnormal trait distributions within their study cohorts. By integrating these methods into your R workflows, you can better resolve population heterogeneity and detect measurement noninvariance across diverse genetic samples. This approach allows for more robust inference when linking genetic markers to complex, multi-layered phenotypes.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/latent-variable-mixture-models) to register, and we would appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Learn Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities in R
messages.subject_clean: Learn Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Enhance your research capabilities with our comprehensive, self-paced training on advanced multivariate data analysis. Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities led by Oliver Miler (Northwest Indian Fisheries Commission) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This on-demand seminar provides researchers with the practical skills needed to conduct multivariate analysis of complex biological datasets using R. Members of the genetics community can apply these advanced analytical workflows to model multi-locus genetic patterns and explore complex gene-environment interactions. By mastering these multivariate techniques and visualization tools, you can uncover hidden structures in your genomic and ecological data to publish more robust research findings.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/multivariate-analysis-of-biotic-communit-1) online at your convenience and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] AI in Healthcare for Research and Practice - On-Demand Research Seminar
messages.subject_clean: AI in Healthcare for Research and Practice - On-Demand Research Seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Enhance your research capabilities with our comprehensive on-demand training programs. AI in Healthcare for Research and Practice led by Arvind Rao (School of Public Health, University of Michigan) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. Researchers analyzing genetic data can apply these advanced AI methodologies to improve diagnostic modeling and pattern recognition in complex genomic datasets. By mastering these machine learning and image processing frameworks, you can better integrate clinical phenotypes with genetic markers in your R-based workflows. This on-demand seminar provides the foundational skills needed to validate AI models and collaborate effectively on interdisciplinary biomedical projects.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-in-healthcare-for-research-and-practi-1) at your own pace and please share this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] (no subject)
messages.body_plain:
Are you using R and Bioconductor for genomic data analysis and interested in creating your own tools?
 
Join our online course ?Developing R/Bioconductor Packages for Genomics? (6?10 July) and learn how to transform your code into robust, reusable, and shareable packages.
 
This five-day hands-on workshop will introduce the principles and best practices of R and Bioconductor package development, combining lectures, demonstrations, and practical exercises. You will learn how to build, document, test, maintain, and disseminate packages within the Bioconductor ecosystem.
 
During the course, you will learn how to:
Structure and develop R packages using best practices
 
Write, document, and test functions
 
Manage dependencies, versions, and continuous integration
 
Create custom Bioconductor objects and include data in packages
 
Build package vignettes and support websites
 
Submit, release, and maintain Bioconductor packages
The course is designed for biologists and bioinformaticians who already have some experience with R and want to expand their programming skills by developing tools that can be shared with the research community.
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Last chance: Practical Meta-Analysis for Beginners
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Practical Meta-Analysis for Beginners
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Last chance - Enhance your statistical toolkit with our upcoming livestream seminar starting next week. Practical Meta-Analysis for Beginners is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 22 and 24 with Centre for Applied Statistics Courses (CASC) (University College London). By mastering the fundamentals of meta-analysis, genetic researchers can systematically synthesize diverse study results to identify robust genetic markers and patterns. This workshop equips you with the statistical tools to pool effect sizes and address publication bias, which are critical for validating findings across different genomic datasets. You will also learn to implement these meta-analytic workflows directly in R, allowing you to integrate these methods seamlessly into your existing genetic analysis pipelines.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/practical-meta-analysis-for-beginners-1) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] AI for Data Visualization and Dashboards
messages.subject_clean: AI for Data Visualization and Dashboards
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-GENETICS members

Effectively communicating complex scientific insights is essential for advancing modern research. AI for Data Visualization and Dashboards led by Hadi Fariborzi (Bissett School of Business, Mount Royal University, Calgary) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. Geneticists can leverage AI-assisted workflows in R and other platforms to rapidly build publication-ready visualizations of complex genomic datasets and spatial trends. This on-demand seminar teaches you how to use AI copilots to automate data cleaning, generate reproducible code, and design interactive dashboards that make your genetic findings more accessible to reviewers and collaborators. By mastering these AI-driven visualization techniques, you can streamline your analysis pipelines and present multi-dimensional biological data with greater clarity and impact.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-for-data-visualization-and-dashboards-1) on demand at your own pace, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
